questions.question: What is the default value of the `overflow` property?
questions.explanation: The default value of overflow is visible, which allows content to render outside the element's box without clipping.

questions.question: What does `overflow: auto` do?
choices: Shows scrollbars only when content overflows
Automatically resizes the element to fit content
questions.explanation: overflow: auto only displays scrollbars when the content actually overflows the element's box.

questions.question: Which property would you use to show an ellipsis (...) when text overflows a single line?
questions.explanation: text-overflow: ellipsis displays an ellipsis when text overflows. It also requires overflow: hidden and white-space: nowrap to work.

questions.question: What does `overflow-x: scroll` combined with `overflow-y: hidden` produce?
choices: A horizontal scrollbar only, vertical overflow is clipped
A vertical scrollbar only, horizontal overflow is clipped
questions.explanation: overflow-x: scroll adds a horizontal scrollbar, while overflow-y: hidden clips any content that overflows vertically.

questions.question: Setting `overflow: ___` clips overflowing content and does not provide a scrollbar.
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden clips content that extends beyond the element's box without providing any way to scroll to it.

questions.question: The ___ property controls overflow behavior on the horizontal axis only.
questions.explanation: overflow-x controls how content that overflows the element's left and right edges is handled.
